data.content:
Plan:
1. Refactor the retake dialog contract in `src/components/retake-dialog.tsx` so the footer exposes two explicit submit paths: `Stage` and `Retake Now`. Keep both actions sharing the same note textarea and pending/disabled handling, but pass the selected action back to the caller instead of a bare notes string.
2. In `src/components/retakes-view.tsx`, extract the existing retake payload construction into a shared helper/mutation entry point that always applies the canonical retake state, regression label, and reject reason/note text first. `Stage` should stop after that mutation succeeds.
3. Add a `Retake Now` follow-up path in `src/components/retakes-view.tsx` that immediately starts a session with the same semantics as queued-screen `Take!`: reuse the same repo resolution, short-circuit to an already-running session for the same beat, and surface start-session failures separately from the state-change success so the beat is still staged even if launch fails.
4. Do not derive ancestor blocking from the retake-candidate subset. RetakesView currently filters fetched beats down to retake-source states, which would miss a rolling grandparent when an intermediate parent is not itself retake-eligible. Instead, keep or derive a complete per-repo beat map from the unfiltered fetch result (or a shared helper built from full beats data) and use that to walk parent chains before `Retake Now` launches `Take!`.
5. To avoid duplicated behavior drifting from `src/app/beats/page.tsx`, extract the rolling-ancestor walk into a shared helper under `src/lib` and have both the main beats page and RetakesView call the same function. That helper should take `parentByBeatId`, `shippingByBeatId`, and the target beat, and return whether any ancestor is currently rolling.
6. Update the user feedback copy in RetakesView so the two actions are distinguishable: success toast for staged-only, success toast for staged-and-started, info toast when a session is already running, and an info/error path when staging succeeded but `Take!` could not launch because of a rolling ancestor or terminal API failure.
7. Test strategy:
   - Add/extend a helper test in `src/lib/__tests__` covering rolling-ancestor detection through multiple generations, including the required regression where a grandparent is rolling and the intermediate parent is absent from the retake-candidate subset.
   - Extend `src/lib/__tests__/retake-lightbox-contract.test.ts` (or add a nearby contract test) to assert the dialog now exposes `Stage` and `Retake Now` instead of a single `ReTake` submit button while still referencing `RETAKE_TARGET_STATE`.
   - Add a focused unit test around the retake action helper in `src/components` or `src/lib` that verifies `Retake Now` performs the update first and only then attempts `startSession`, preserving the staged mutation when session launch fails.
Complexity: medium. Main risks are keeping `Retake Now` behavior aligned with queued-screen `Take!` semantics and avoiding ancestry bugs when retakes are shown across multiple repos. The implementation should keep all ancestry data repo-scoped so a beat never resolves parents from another checkout.
